an1.150:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who declare a non-offense to be an offense are practicing for the harm of many, for the non-*sukha* of many, for the harm, hurt, and *dukkha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.150:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much demerit; they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.151:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who declare an offense to be a non-offense are practicing for the harm of many, for the non-*sukha* of many, for the harm, hurt, and *dukkha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.151:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much demerit; they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.152-159:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who declare a light offense to be a heavy offense … who declare a heavy offense to be a light offense … who declare a serious offense to be a non-serious offense … who declare a non-serious offense to be a serious offense … who declare a remediable offense to be irremediable … who declare an irremediable offense to be remediable … who declare a redressable offense to be non-redressable … who declare a non-redressable offense to be redressable — those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the harm of many, for the non-*sukha* of many, for the harm, hurt, and *dukkha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.152-159:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much demerit; they cause this true *dhamma* to disappear."
an1.160:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who declare a non-offense to be a non-offense are practicing for the welfare of many, for the *sukha* of many, for the benefit, welfare, and *sukha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.160:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much merit; they uphold this true *dhamma*."
an1.161:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who declare an offense to be an offense are practicing for the welfare of many, for the *sukha* of many, for the benefit, welfare, and *sukha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.161:1.2: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much merit; they uphold this true *dhamma*."
an1.162-169:1.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, who declare a light offense to be a light offense … who declare a heavy offense to be a heavy offense … who declare a serious offense to be a serious offense … who declare a non-serious offense to be a non-serious offense … who declare a remediable offense to be remediable … who declare an irremediable offense to be irremediable … who declare a redressable offense to be redressable … who declare a non-redressable offense to be non-redressable —
an1.162-169:1.2: those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, are practicing for the welfare of many, for the *sukha* of many, for the benefit, welfare, and *sukha* of many people, of gods and humans.
an1.162-169:1.3: And those *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhus*, generate much merit; they uphold this true *dhamma*."
